key_points: The poem is about a mysterious, quiet little man who lives in everyone's house.
He is described as being as 'quiet as a mouse'.
No one has ever seen his face, yet he is responsible for all the mischief in the house.
The poem uses a lighthearted and humorous tone to talk about common household accidents.
It shows how family members often blame an 'invisible' person when things go wrong.
examples: When a plate is broken, the 'Funny Man' is the one who did it.
He is blamed for leaving the door ajar (open).
He pulls buttons off shirts and scatters pins on the floor.
He is the reason why the fire goes out or why books are left lying around.
Even the muddy footprints on the carpet are said to be his work.
formulas_or_processes: The Mischief Pattern → Something breaks/gets lost → No one admits to it → Everyone blames 'The Funny Man'.
The Character Look → Quiet + Small + Invisible + Busy.
The Family Rule → If you don't know who did it, it was probably 'Mr. Nobody' (The Funny Man).
exam_tips: Remember the main comparison: He is as 'quiet as a mouse'.
Understand that the 'Funny Man' isn't real—he is a symbol for the mistakes we all make.
The poem is funny because it describes things that happen in every home.
Key theme: Taking responsibility vs. making excuses.
